free_text: Channel cross correlations and intermediate structure
in the 27Al(d,alpha)25Mg and 27Al(d,p)28Al reactions
Department of Physics.
The experiment was carried out with the
7 MV Van de Graaff.
The scattered particles were detected by two
detectors which were fixed at angles = 90 and 160 degr.
with respect to the beam direction.  Diffused
junction detectors (- 140 mum) were used for alpha
particles, while for the detection of protons the
detectors were replaced by surface barrier detectors
(1500 mum and -2000 mum) which were covered by Al foil
of thickness 0.031 mm and 0.022 mm in front of their
windows to avoid interference of the alpha particles.
A third, 500 mum thick surface-barrier detector was
fixed at angle = 40 degr. as a monitor to see the
deuterons scattered elastically.
The 27-Al targets were prepared by vacuum evaporation
and their thickness was about 100 mug/cm2, which
corresponds to an energy loss of about 14 keV for 5 MeV
deuterons.
The absolute cross sections were measured at Ed = 3.5
MeV with elastic deuterons and were in quite good
agreement with the results of Rutherford scattering.
The beam energy resolution was estimated to be
The errors were estimated to be about 15%.
Any analysis is not given.
By CAJAD from fig.2 of J.Phys.,G9(1983)545
